results.title: Senior Data Analytics Manager – Career Empowerment Center | Cal State East Bay
highlights:
Senior Data Analytics Manager – Career Empowerment Center | Cal State East Bay
...
Southern California Edison (SCE)
...
# Senior Data Analytics Manager
...
Become a Senior Data Analytics Manager at Southern California Edison (SCE) and build a better tomorrow. In this job, you’ll be leading a talented and dynamic team that provides centralized data, analytics, and reporting on SCE’s assets and operations to support operational decisions, enable regulatory compliance, and inform data-driven strategies.
...
As a Senior Data Analytics Manager, your work will help power our planet, reduce carbon emissions and create cleaner air for everyone. Are you ready to take on the challenge to help us build the future?
...
- Advises business leaders by providing data-based strategic direction to identify and address business issues and opportunities.
...
- Identifies areas to increase efficiency and automate data analysis processes.
...
- Plans, manages, and controls the activities of a team or teams of analysts that provide business intelligence and strategic planning support for business segments or the company at large.
...
- Leads initiatives to analyze complex business problems and issues using data from internal and external sources.
...
- Brings expertise or identifies subject matter experts in support of multi-functional efforts to identify, interpret, and produce recommendations and plans based on company and external data analysis.
...
- Oversees the activities of defining data requirements, data collection, processing, cleaning, analysis, modeling, and visualization.
...
- Examines and identifies data patterns and trends to help answer business questions and improve decision-making.
...
- Provides input for complex data science and big data mining projects.
...
- Five or more years of experience managing or supervising an organization or unit, or five or more years of project-management experience.
- Experience with data analysis and data systems and software.
...
- Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, data analytics, or a closely related science and engineering field.
...
- Five or more years of hands-on experience working in data analytics roles, including experience with ETL processes and navigating ambiguity within available data sources.
...
- Hands-on experiences with languages like Python, SQL, or SAS and SAP as well familiarity with cloud computing environments (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and data visualization tools like SAP Datasphere or Power BI.
...
- Experience designing and maintaining processes to manage high volumes of analytical requests, ensuring timely delivery and consistently high-quality outputs.
...
- Experience overseeing responses to regulatory data requests from external agencies (e.g., Energy Safety, SED) and compliance reporting, including assigning work, reviewing the accuracy of the outputs, meeting tight deadlines and ensuring consistency with prior submissions.
...
- Experience driving process improvements, automation, and standardization of analytics workflows to improve efficiency, reduce manual effort, and enhance data quality
...
- Experience providing technical direction and subject matter expertise on asset data, asset inspections, notifications, work orders and compliance requirements.
...
The people at SCE don’t just keep the lights on. Our mission is so much bigger. We’re fueling the kind of innovation that’s changing an entire industry, and quite possibly the planet. Join us and create a future with cleaner energy, while providing our customers with the safety and reliability they demand. At SCE, you’ll have a chance to grow personally and professionally, making a real impact in Southern California and around the world.

results.title: AI Engineer - Forward Deployed at Edison Scientific
highlights:
AI Engineer - Forward Deployed at Edison Scientific
...
Jobs at Edison Scientific
...
# AI Engineer - Forward Deployed
...
at Edison Scientific • Full-time
...
Edison Scientific builds and commercializes AI agents for science. Scientific discovery moves too slowly, and autonomous AI agents are how we intend to fix that. We're assembling a team of top researchers and engineers across AI and biology to build an AI scientist.
...
As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you'll be embedded directly with one of the world's leading science R&D organizations – taking full ownership of how Edison's AI agents create value in their environment. You'll need to deeply understand their science, their workflows, and their pain points, then determine where our platform can have the most impact.
...
This isn't remote support or implementation consulting. You'll be on-site, building relationships with scientists and research leaders, identifying the highest-leverage problems, and shipping solutions in fast cycles – often end-to-end. You'll operate with a high degree of independence and serve as the bridge between what our platform can do and what the customer needs it to do, feeding insights back to our product and engineering teams to shape the roadmap.
...
### Responsibilities
...
Be embedded at a client site, understand their research workflows deeply, and take responsibility for maximizing the value Edison delivers to that organization.
...
Map the customer's scientific and operational landscape to identify where AI agents can have the most impact – not just where they ask for help, but where the real leverage is.
...
Design, implement, and iterate on production-ready integrations in fast cycles, often owning features end-to-end from architecture to rollout.
...
Troubleshoot issues in real time, ensure reliability and adoption, and be the go-to technical contact for AI within the client organization.
...
Capture pain points, unmet needs, and product gaps and work with our internal Applied AI and engineering teams to shape future capabilities.
...
2+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a track record of shipping production-grade software in fast-paced or ambiguous environments.
...
Strong problem-solving skills and comfort navigating complex systems, debugging issues in real time, and owning features end-to-end.
...
Proficiency in Python and/or TypeScript/JavaScript.
...
Strong communication and relationship-building skills – able to earn trust with scientists, executives, and engineering teams.
...
Willingness to learn new scientific domains quickly and adapt to rapidly evolving priorities.
...
### Bonus points for:
...
Experience in life sciences, pharma, biotech, or scientific R&D – either as a researcher, consultant, or engineer working in these environments.
...
Experience working with AI/ML systems, LLM-powered applications, or agent architectures.
...
Background as a technical founder, forward-deployed engineer, or management consultant.
...
A science degree or research experience alongside your engineering skills.
...
$220,000 - $350,000 • Offers equity
...
San Francisco; Boston; New York; Remote available (U.S. Only)

results.title: Grid SCADA Historian Specialist – Career & Internship Center | University of Washington
highlights:
Southern California Edison (SCE)
...
# Grid SCADA Historian Specialist
...
Become a Grid SCADA Historian Specialist at Southern California Edison (SCE) and build a better tomorrow. In this job, you’ll be part of a team responsible for maintaining some of the most critical applications across the company. You will be responsible for supporting the Distribution Aveva Pi Historian. This system is a near real-time data management platform that collects, stores, and visualizes data from industrial processes and equipment across the electric grid. In this job, you will support situational awareness and critical analytics for SCE’s distribution system.
...
- Translate business needs into operationally supportable designs.
- Plan and execute both structured and unstructured testing.
- Develop PI analytics and reporting to meet Transmission & Distribution (T&D) client requirements.
...
- Collaborate with clients, IT teams, and vendors to support the operation and maintenance of the existing PI Historian system.
...
- Maintain and enhance scripts supporting the GridMS PI Historian environment.
- Ensure all deliverables comply with SCE standards, including regulatory and cybersecurity requirements.
...
- Provides operations support, troubleshooting, integration testing and user training. Performs technical analysis to define grid application requirements and updates.
...
- Monitors and analyzes energy usage data to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for optimization.
...
- Supports the testing and implementation of new grid application releases.
...
- Identifies trends and root causes of issues, determines solutions, and makes recommendations for management consideration.
...
- Updates, monitors and maintains the reliability of the grid application advance apps, and control application.
...
- Identifies and implements actions plans to prevent future system issues.
...
- Configures and modifies grid application databases and ensures that all telemetry and controls are available and accurate.
...
- Provides engineering support to grid operations by resolving complex issues via implementation of state-of- the art and new technologies.
...
- Updates, monitors project KPIs and maintains power system modeling, state estimator, contingency analysis and power flow analysis supporting real-time operations.
...
- Three or more years of experience in designing, building, implementing, maintaining, and supporting complex utility, real-time or enterprise systems, or as a user of a utility grid application.
...
- Relevant IT or engineering experience focused on data or analytics solutions.
- Experience implementing or administering AVEVEI PI Systems, including its architecture and change management process.
- Development and scripting experience with shell scripts, .NET, and C#
- Experience with SCADA systems.
- Background in data analysis and report development.
- Experience with SQL.
...
The people at SCE don’t just keep the lights on. Our mission is so much bigger. We’re fueling the kind of innovation that’s changing an entire industry, and quite possibly the planet. Join us and create a future with cleaner energy, while providing our customers with the safety and reliability they demand. At SCE, you’ll have a chance to grow personally and professionally, making a real impact in Southern California and around the world.

results.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer at Jobgether - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Develop frameworks for
...
and monitoring AI
...
, including reasoning
...
, consistency, performance, and fairness.
...
- Translate complex machine learning and AI requirements into structured technical tasks and actionable development plans.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

Achieved significant career highlights to date including establishing an Enterprise API Management platform on-premises and Azure Cloud to enable the enterprise Digital Utility Initiative. Certified in Data Science and Machine Learning with a background in Google and Azure Cloud, API Management, and DevOps. Experienced in establishing data engineering and integration pipelines in Multi-Cloud environments Collaborative leader with excellent people skills, and verbal and communication skills.

results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
...
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
...
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
...
reduction, or product differentiation).
...
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
...
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
...
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).
...

...
Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.
...
Experience with responsible AI frameworks, model governance, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
...
Track record of building AI developer platforms or shared ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams.
